key_insight: Atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potentials can bring screw-dislocation predictions in bcc refractory metals much closer to DFT, but transferability depends strongly on what data are included and on the specific element.
engineering_relevance: More accurate dislocation models can improve prediction of flow stress and plasticity in high-temperature refractory metals used in extreme environments.
method: Extended an existing DFT database for V, Nb, Ta, Mo, and W
Trained a set of ACE interatomic potentials
Benchmarked Peierls barriers, glide planes, and kink-pair activation enthalpies against DFT and analytic models
evidence_signal: The ACE models achieve near-DFT accuracy for Mo and W and substantial improvements for V, Nb, and Ta, including reliable kink-pair enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not enough to validate dislocation glide accuracy, so database composition and broader defect benchmarking remain critical.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
